task_description: Search for an artist, browse to their albums, and play a specific song.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the empty white Google search/address input area, specifically on the blank field itself rather than on an icon or shortcut. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to a new tab page in Chinese, with the large Google logo centered above the long rounded search box. The click target is a UI control in the page’s main content area: the central search field.
caption.think: The user likely wants to place the text cursor into the Google search box so they can begin entering a search query or web address.
caption.action: Click the central Google search box.
caption.expectation: The search field should become active with a blinking text cursor, ready for typing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view centers on the active Google search field, which now contains the typed text "spotify" with the text cursor at the end. A drop-down suggestion panel is open directly beneath it, showing a first suggestion for searching Spotify on Google and a result with the green Spotify circle logo and the label "Spotify". In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the Google homepage in Chinese, with the large Google logo above the centered rounded search box and the suggestions list expanded below it.
caption.think: The user is entering the search term SPOTIFY into the Google search field to look up Spotify, likely preparing to submit the search or choose a suggestion.
caption.action: Type "SPOTIFY" into the central Google search box, appending it in the active field.
caption.expectation: The search box should display the completed Spotify query, and Google autocomplete suggestions related to Spotify should remain visible or update beneath the field.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Google search field area with a semitransparent red X over the autocomplete dropdown region directly beneath the search box. The visible text includes the typed query "spotify" and the top highlighted suggestion "spotify - Google 搜索". In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the Google homepage in Chinese, with the centered Google logo, the rounded search box containing "spotify", and the suggestion list expanded below it.
caption.think: The user has finished entering the search term and wants to submit the current Google search for Spotify rather than click a specific suggestion manually.
caption.expectation: Google should submit the search and load the search results page for "spotify".

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X placed directly on the purple, underlined first search result title text, "Spotify - Web Player: Music for everyone." This is a clickable search-result link on the page content, not a toolbar control or blank area. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying Google results for "spotify" in Chinese, and this top result appears under the official Spotify site entry with the green Spotify logo and URL.
caption.think: The user wants to open the main official Spotify web player result from the Google search results.
caption.action: Click the first search result link, "Spotify - Web Player: Music for everyone."
caption.expectation: The Spotify result should be selected, and Chrome should begin loading the Spotify website or web player page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on Spotify's top search field, specifically over the placeholder text in Chinese that reads "想播放什么?" inside a rounded dark search bar with a magnifying-glass icon on the left and keyboard shortcut badges on the right. This is a UI control in the top navigation area. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to the Spotify Web Player home page in Chinese, with recommendation tiles in the main content area, the left sidebar library panel, and the currently playing track visible in the bottom player bar.
caption.think: The user has opened Spotify and now wants to activate the search box so they can search for music, an artist, or other content.
caption.action: Click the Spotify search field labeled "想播放什么?" in the top bar.
caption.expectation: The search field should become active and ready for text entry, likely showing a text cursor and focusing the search interface.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view shows Spotify’s top search bar already active, with a magnifying-glass icon on the left and the text being entered visible as “taylor swif”. Beneath it, the autocomplete panel is open and shows matching suggestions including “taylor swift,” “taylor swift playlist,” “taylor swift top 50 songs,” and artist/song results such as “Taylor Swift” and “The Fate of Ophelia.” In the full-screen view, this is the Spotify Web Player in Chinese inside Google Chrome, with the home page content dimmed behind the search suggestions, the left sidebar library area, recommendation cards in the center, and the current track panel on the right.
caption.think: The user is searching Spotify for Taylor Swift and is in the middle of entering the artist name into the search field, continuing the text input rather than selecting a suggestion yet.
caption.action: Type “TAYLOR SWIFT” into the active Spotify search field, completing or replacing the current partial query with the full artist name.
caption.expectation: The search text should update to the full query “taylor swift,” and the suggestion list should refresh to show results more specifically matched to Taylor Swift.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows Spotify’s rounded dark search bar at the top with a magnifying-glass icon on the left and the query text “taylor swift” fully entered. A semitransparent red X is visible over the text, but the stated action is a keyboard action. Directly below, the autocomplete dropdown is open with suggestions such as “taylor swift,” “taylor swift top 50 songs,” and “taylor swift playlist.” In the full-screen view, Spotify Web Player is open in Chrome in Chinese, with the search suggestions panel expanded over the home page recommendations and the search field still focused.
caption.think: The user has finished typing the artist name and now wants to submit the search rather than click a suggestion, so pressing Enter should execute the search for Taylor Swift.
caption.action: Press Enter to submit the search for “taylor swift.”
caption.expectation: Spotify should load the search results page for Taylor Swift, replacing the suggestions dropdown with matching results such as the artist page, songs, albums, and playlists.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X over a plain dark gray/black area with no visible button, icon, text label, or card content directly under it; the lower-left edge only shows the tail end of large white text from nearby content, indicating the target is a blank region rather than a control. In the full-screen view, Spotify search results for “taylor swift” are open in Chrome. The center panel shows the artist result card for Taylor Swift and a songs list, while the red X sits in the empty dark background area above the results content, below the browser toolbar and near the top center of the Spotify page. This is a blank region on the canvas/content area, not a toolbar control or object.
caption.think: The user likely wants to dismiss focus from the search field or simply place focus on the results page without activating any specific result or control.
caption.action: Click the blank dark background area in the top-center of the Spotify results page.
caption.expectation: Focus should move away from any active text field or highlighted control, with no major navigation change; the page should remain on the Taylor Swift search results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the full Spotify artist page rather than a specific control, with no semitransparent red X. The page header displays Taylor Swift’s large banner image and name, the green Play button, a Follow button, and the start of the popular songs list. In the full-screen context, Spotify Web Player is open in Chrome in Chinese, with the main center content on Taylor Swift’s artist page, a right sidebar showing the currently playing track “Father Figure” by Taylor Swift and an “关于艺人” section below, and the left library sidebar visible. This indicates a mouse-wheel scroll on the main page content area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to move further down Taylor Swift’s artist page to see more content below the top banner and popular tracks section.
caption.action: Scroll down on the Spotify artist page’s main content area.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward, with the large header and top of the popular tracks section sliding up to reveal more of the artist page below.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the row of pill-shaped category tabs under the heading ‘唱片目录’. The semitransparent red X is centered on the middle dark tab labeled ‘专辑’, between the lighter ‘热门新歌’ tab on the left and ‘单曲和 EP’ on the right. This is a UI control tab, not a blank area or canvas object. In the full-screen context, Chrome is open to Taylor Swift’s Spotify artist page, with the discography section visible and album covers shown below these category filters.
caption.think: The user likely wants to filter the discography to show Taylor Swift’s albums only.
caption.action: Click the ‘专辑’ tab in the discography filter row.
caption.expectation: The discography section should switch to the albums view, updating the visible releases to album items and marking the ‘专辑’ tab as selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a song row in Spotify’s track list. The semitransparent red X is centered on the row for “Father Figure,” with the song title in green and “Taylor Swift” shown beneath it; a small square explicit-content badge appears before the artist name. A tooltip reads “播放 Taylor Swift 的 Father Figure,” confirming this is the playable track row. In the full-screen context, Chrome is displaying the Spotify album page for “The Life of a Showgirl + Acoustic Collection,” and the track list is visible in the center with “Father Figure” as one of the songs. This is a canvas object/list item rather than a toolbar control or blank area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to select and play the specific track “Father Figure” from the album track list.
caption.expectation: The “Father Figure” song should become the active selected track and begin playing immediately, with Spotify updating the now-playing area and playback state.
